title: fix: track audio-second prices in Tinybird
author: ElliotEtag
pr_body_excerpt:
- Adds prompt and completion audio-second registry price rates to generation-event pricing payloads.
- Adds `token_price_prompt_audio_seconds` and `token_price_completion_audio_seconds` to the Tinybird datasource.
- Adds a focused regression test proving both values are preserved.
- Leaves model prices, costs, multipliers, usage calculations, canonical names, aliases, and other telemetry unchanged.

Checks:
- `npm run decrypt-vars`
- `npx vitest run test/track-image.test.ts --testNamePattern="audio-second event prices are preserved"`
- `npx biome check --write shared/schemas/generation-event.ts enter.pollinations.ai/test/track-image.test.ts`
- `tb fmt --dry-run datasources/generation_event.datasource`

The staging deployment check was skipped because no workspace-scoped staging token was available. Deployment to both Tinybird workspaces remains required after review and explicit approval.

gist.headline: Audio prices find their ledger
gist.blurb: The accounting garden now keeps audio-second rates alongside the rest of each generation event. Two small fields, fewer mysterious gaps in the hive ledger.
gist.summary: Generation-event pricing payloads and the Tinybird generation_event datasource now preserve prompt and completion audio-second registry rates in token_price_prompt_audio_seconds and token_price_completion_audio_seconds.
gist.impact: Audio generation telemetry can now report both prompt and completion per-second prices accurately while leaving existing model prices, costs, multipliers, usage calculations, names, aliases, and other fields unchanged.
gist.visual_concept: A cozy garden-accounting scene: Polly records two audio-duration price entries in a glowing retro ledger beside a Tinybird-inspired pixel bird and a terminal showing prompt and completion audio seconds. Small code-plants and labeled jars represent the newly preserved telemetry fields, with the monitor robot helping verify both entries.
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene in a warm garden workshop where Polly, the cute pixel art bee mascot with a yellow body, dark brown horizontal stripes, black oval eyes, pink rectangular blush, light blue rounded wings, and a tiny accountant visor, carefully writes two glowing entries labeled “PROMPT s” and “COMPLETION s” into a retro ledger beside a friendly tiny pixel bird and CRT terminal; code-plants sprout from lime green (#ecf874) pots while a helpful monitor robot checks the numbers. Cozy 8-bit pixel art aesthetic, soft lime green (#ecf874) and pastel gradient background, warm lighting, retro gaming vibes like Stardew Valley or A Short Hike, subtle CRT glow, nostalgic but beautiful.
